TWEET 1
$650 billion. That's what Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft will spend on AI infrastructure in 2026 — up 58% from last year. Bridgewater calls it a "more dangerous phase." Here's what executives need to understand ->

---

TWEET 2
The breakdown per Bridgewater/Reuters: Amazon ~$200B, Alphabet ~$185B, Microsoft ~$145B, Meta $115-135B. This isn't R&D — it's physical infrastructure. Data centers. Chips. Power. The AI supply chain is being built at nation-state scale.

---

TWEET 3
Bridgewater co-CIO Greg Jensen flagged the risk: exponentially rising capex plus growing reliance on outside capital. Translation — the AI arms race now has real financial gravity. Margins will tighten. Winners will separate from followers fast.

---

TWEET 4
For every CEO outside Big Tech: $650B in infra means compute gets cheaper, AI-as-a-service gets more competitive, and the barrier to deploying production AI drops. The question isn't whether to adopt — it's whether your roadmap matches the pace.

---

TWEET 5
The window for treating AI as a pilot project is closing. When infrastructure spend exceeds the GDP of most countries in a single year, "wait and see" becomes the most expensive strategy on the table.

---

TWEET 6

## Fact-check notes
- Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft expected to collectively invest ~$650 billion in AI infrastructure in 2026, up from $410 billion in 2025 — per Bridgewater Associates analysis reported by Reuters on Feb 23, 2026
- Bridgewater co-CIO Greg Jensen described AI boom as entering a 'more dangerous phase' marked by exponentially rising physical infrastructure investments and growing reliance on outside capital — Reuters, Feb 23, 2026
- Individual pledges reported by MML Studio: Amazon ~$200B, Alphabet ~$185B, Microsoft ~$145B, Meta $115-135B for AI data centers in 2026
